$comment: Per-language sandbox resource profiles. Each profile tunes the rlimit triple (memMB, cpuSeconds, wallSeconds) to the realistic working-set of that toolchain. Compiled native (c/cpp/go/rust) gets aggressive 2s CPU because their solutions JIT to native; JVM languages (java) get 8s CPU + 15s wall because the JVM spends 1-3s warming up before the user code even runs. Anything not listed falls back to default. Memory is the virtual-address ceiling enforced via --rlimit_as.
